misconceptionSummary: Students order by number of decimal places — fewer places means smaller.

steps.narration: The student put zero point three seven five last because it has the most digits. But digit count is irrelevant — only column values matter.
Pad all three to three decimal places. Now the tenths column is clear: five, four, three. Three is the smallest tenths digit, so zero point three seven five comes first.
The correct order is zero point three seven five, then zero point four eight, then zero point five. More digits made it smaller here, not larger.
Zero point three seven five is the smallest. Pad with zeros, trust the columns — never let digit count fool you.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: assuming more decimal digits means a larger number. Zero point three seven five has three decimal places but is the smallest here — the tenths digit three is less than four or five.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Digit count is a trap. Pad to equal length and compare the tenths column first — that column decides the order.
